fragility_points: A quick selloff toward 70k or below would compress the cushion fast.
Any Binance-specific print anomaly near the settlement minute could matter disproportionately.
key_assumptions: Current Binance spot and recent range are informative for the next six days.
No major shock or Binance-specific dislocation occurs before the settlement minute.
The market is more likely mildly overconfident than directionally wrong.
main_logical_chain: Market implies about 93.5-94% Yes because current Binance spot is comfortably above 68k.
Direct Binance data supports a strong Yes lean because recent spot and range have stayed above the strike.
But the contract is narrow and short-horizon crypto downside tails are not negligible.
So the correct update is high-probability Yes, but modestly below market at 88%.
main_thesis: Market is directionally right that >68k is likely, but 93.5-94% looks somewhat overconfident given six-day BTC volatility and the exact Binance noon-ET minute-close condition.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone ... has a final 'Close' price higher than ... 68,000.
source_quality_view: Primary contract source and settlement venue data are strong, but evidence independence is only medium and no authoritative future-settling source exists yet.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still move 8-10% in six days.
Resolution depends on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, adding timing and venue-specific risk.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT traded around 74.2k during the run, about 9.2% above the 68k strike.
Recent Binance daily lows in the sampled week stayed above roughly 70.5k.
Polymarket strike ladder is internally coherent, supporting an efficient-summary interpretation.
timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on April 20, 2026 at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close, so date/time precision is material.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact short-horizon downside-tail frequency was not quantified in a formal base-rate study.
Noon-ET candle operational mapping appears clear from rules but still matters mechanically.
what_would_change_view: A move of Binance BTC/USDT back near 70k, a new macro/crypto shock, or evidence of Binance-specific settlement noise would lower the estimate; continued stable trading above 72k would raise it.
